sections.executive_summary:
This proposal covers the design and deployment of a custom AI research agent for [Company Name] — an autonomous system that monitors your competitive landscape, compiles intelligence from multiple sources, and delivers a structured briefing to your team on a defined schedule. Instead of a team member spending 3–5 hours per week on competitive research, this agent does it overnight.

Project investment: $12,500. Timeline: 6 weeks. Optional hosting and maintenance: $2,000/month.
sections.problem_statement:
Competitive intelligence at [Company Name] is currently reactive. Someone thinks to check a competitor's website when there's a sales conversation, or reads about an industry development on LinkedIn. There's no systematic process for monitoring what's happening in your market.

This matters more than it used to. Your competitors are shipping product updates, pricing changes, new partnerships, new hires, and new messaging. Each of these is a signal. A competitor's new hire in enterprise sales signals an upmarket move. A pricing page change signals competitive pressure. A new partnership signals a strategic direction. Catching these signals early means you can respond before they become problems.

The alternative — a dedicated analyst doing this manually — costs $50,000–$80,000/year and produces research with the same cadence as their availability. An AI agent does it continuously, at a consistent quality, and at a fraction of the cost.
sections.proposed_solution:
**The Research Agent Architecture:**

**Data Sources (configured to your competitive landscape):**
- Competitor websites (change monitoring)
- Company news and press releases
- LinkedIn activity (competitor hires, job postings, executive posts)
- Review sites (G2, Capterra, Trustpilot — competitor reviews and your reviews)
- Patent filings if relevant
- Regulatory filings if relevant
- Industry news sources and publications
- Social media mentions

**Agent Behavior:**
The agent runs on a defined schedule (daily or weekly). It:
1. Pulls new data from each source
2. Identifies what's changed or new since last run
3. Analyzes significance of changes using a scoring rubric you approve
4. Generates a structured intelligence brief: Executive Summary, Key Developments by Competitor, Strategic Implications, Recommended Actions
5. Delivers the brief via email and/or Slack
6. Stores all raw data and analysis in a structured database for trend analysis over time

**Human-in-the-Loop:**
The agent flags high-significance items for immediate notification, not just the weekly brief. If a competitor announces a major funding round or a direct pricing attack, you know within hours — not at the next weekly cycle.

**Customization:**
The intelligence rubric is built with you — what matters most to [Company Name]. Pricing changes? Product updates? Geographic expansion? Enterprise customer wins? The agent pays attention to what you care about.
sections.deliverables_and_timeline:
**Week 1–2:**
- Intelligence scope definition (which competitors, which signals, which sources)
- Source configuration and data pipeline build
- Agent architecture design and approval

**Week 3–4:**
- Agent build: data ingestion, analysis logic, report generation
- Testing with historical data
- Brief template design and approval

**Week 5:**
- Live run with real data
- Calibration: adjusting signal scoring based on your feedback
- Delivery channel setup (email + Slack)

**Week 6:**
- QA and final tuning
- Handover and documentation
- First full production brief delivered

**Post-launch:**
- 30-day monitoring and optimization included
- Optional: $2,000/month for ongoing hosting, infrastructure management, and quarterly calibration updates
sections.investment:
**Project Total: $12,500**

Includes: scope definition, data pipeline build, agent development, testing, calibration, deployment, documentation, and 30-day post-launch support.

**What's included in the $2,000/month optional maintenance:**
- Agent hosting and infrastructure
- New source additions (up to 2/month)
- Monthly calibration review
- Quarterly strategy call to review intelligence outputs and adjust priorities

**Payment:** 50% on signing ($6,250), 50% on agent go-live.

**ROI Context:**
A competitive intelligence analyst costs $60,000–$90,000/year and works 40 hours/week on more than just this task. This agent runs 24/7, monitors more sources than a human could, and costs $24,000–$36,000 less per year when running on the maintenance plan. The project pays for itself within the first 2–3 months.
sections.team: [Your Name] as agent architect and technical lead. Background in AI agent development using LangChain, LangGraph, and custom API pipelines. References from similar competitive intelligence agent deployments available.
sections.next_steps:
1. Confirm project scope: which competitors (typically 3–5 direct, 5–10 indirect), which source categories matter most
2. Sign service agreement
3. Pay 50% deposit ($6,250)
4. Scope definition call: 90 minutes — mapping your competitive landscape and intelligence priorities
5. Project kickoff: [proposed date]

sections.executive_summary:
This proposal covers the deployment of a custom AI outbound prospecting agent for [Company Name] — an autonomous system that identifies qualified prospects, researches them, drafts personalized outreach, and queues it for rep review and sending. Your sales reps focus on conversations; the agent handles everything before the reply.

Project investment: $18,500. Timeline: 8 weeks.
sections.problem_statement:
Your sales team's biggest bottleneck isn't closing — it's prospecting. Finding qualified prospects, researching them enough to write a relevant email, and actually writing that email takes 2–3 hours per qualified prospect. At that rate, even a dedicated SDR maxes out at 20–30 personalized touches per day.

The quality ceiling is also real. A rep who's been doing research for 4 hours is less thorough on the 10th prospect than the 1st. An AI agent is equally thorough on the 1,000th prospect as the first.
sections.proposed_solution:
A three-stage prospecting agent:

**Stage 1: Prospect Identification**
The agent continuously finds new prospects matching your ICP using configured data sources — LinkedIn, Apollo, industry databases, event attendee lists, job posting signals. It scores each prospect on fit criteria and adds qualifying ones to a research queue.

**Stage 2: Deep Research & Personalization Context**
For each prospect in the queue, the agent compiles: company overview, recent news, LinkedIn activity, tech stack (via BuiltWith or similar), hiring signals, competitor usage, and any relevant trigger events. It generates a personalization brief — 3–5 specific hooks for outreach.

**Stage 3: Outreach Drafting**
Using the personalization brief and your approved message frameworks, the agent drafts a personalized outreach email (and optionally a LinkedIn connection note). The draft lands in a review queue where the rep reads it, edits if desired, and sends with one click. Average review time: 2–3 minutes per prospect versus 15–20 minutes manually.

All activity logged to CRM automatically.
sections.deliverables_and_timeline:
Weeks 1–2: ICP definition, source configuration, agent architecture
Weeks 3–4: Research pipeline build and testing
Weeks 5–6: Outreach drafting logic, message framework development, rep queue UI
Week 7: Full integration, CRM connection, QA
Week 8: Live launch, monitoring, calibration
sections.investment: $18,500 total. 50/50 payment. Optional ongoing maintenance at $3,000/month.
sections.next_steps: ICP definition session required before scoping is finalized. Schedule a 60-minute intake call.

templates.variant: Operations Monitoring Agent — Anomaly Detection & Response

sections.executive_summary:
This proposal covers deployment of an operations monitoring AI agent for [Company Name] — an autonomous system that watches your key operational metrics, detects anomalies, diagnoses probable causes, and takes predefined response actions (or escalates to a human) without waiting to be asked.

Project investment: $15,000. Timeline: 7 weeks.
sections.problem_statement: Operational issues at [Company Name] are discovered late. A server goes down and customers notice before the team does. A payment processing error runs for 3 hours before someone spots it. An inventory item goes out of stock and orders keep coming in. Each of these is preventable with real-time monitoring — but your team can't watch 15 dashboards simultaneously.
sections.proposed_solution:
An autonomous monitoring agent that watches your key metrics across systems, detects deviations from baseline, assesses severity, and either takes a predefined response action (restart a service, send a notification, update a status page) or escalates with a full diagnostic brief — all within minutes of detecting an issue.

Monitoring scope examples: application uptime, payment processing success rates, inventory levels, customer support queue depth, marketing spend pacing, key SaaS metric thresholds. Configured to your specific operations.
sections.deliverables_and_timeline:
Weeks 1–2: Operations audit, metric definition, baseline calculation
Weeks 3–4: Monitoring pipeline build and anomaly detection logic
Weeks 5–6: Response action configuration, escalation paths, alert design
Week 7: QA, deployment, documentation
sections.next_steps: Define scope: which systems and metrics matter most. Operations audit call: 90 minutes.
